prompt:
SRE-audit ticket. Compliance wants every Atlas project event (cluster creates, membership joins, host alarms) mirrored into our audit pipeline with a durable checkpoint, and the current cron script is a disaster: it re-ships the same events whenever pages shift underneath it, it advances its checkpoint BEFORE the pipeline accepted the batch (we lost a day of events when Kafka was down), and it treats an empty page as an error. Build the replacement in plain JDK Java (java.net.http is fine, no Maven, no jars) as sibling classes in this directory — I'd expect an AtlasClient, an EventExporter, the small value/exception types, split however makes sense. TestMain.java is the acceptance harness (runs via the multi-file source launcher: java TestMain.java).

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official MongoDB documentation for the Atlas Administration API v2 events and alerts resources — at minimum the return-events-from-one-project operation page plus the Atlas API reference conventions for pagination links, empty collections, and the error envelope. Only first-party MongoDB pages count as sources (mongodb.com/docs, the Atlas Admin API v2 reference, or MongoDB's own published API spec). Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If live docs disagree with those fixtures, flag the conflict in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface TestMain compiles against:
- AtlasClient(String baseUrl, String token) — every request sends Bearer auth plus the documented dated Accept media type; non-2xx responses raise AtlasApiException (status(), errorCode(), detail(), reason()) decoded from the documented application/json error envelope; exception text never contains the token.
- List<AtlasAlert> AtlasClient.alerts(String groupId, String status) — the documented alerts operation with its status filter; reject any status outside the documented enum with IllegalArgumentException before any HTTP happens. AtlasAlert exposes id(), alertConfigId(), eventTypeName(), status(), created(), updated().
- interface EventSink { void deliver(java.util.List<AtlasEvent> events); } and AtlasEvent exposing id(), eventTypeName(), created(), username() (null when Atlas omits it).
- EventExporter(AtlasClient client, java.nio.file.Path checkpointFile) with ExportReport export(String groupId, java.util.List<String> eventTypes, EventSink sink); ExportReport exposes delivered() and totalCount().
- export() queries the documented events operation: one repeated eventType parameter per requested type (caller order), then minDate taken from the checkpoint file when it exists (mind reserved-character encoding — the tests pin the exact query string), then itemsPerPage=100, includeCount=true, pageNum=1. Scan ALL pages by following the documented next links verbatim; dedupe events across page drift by their documented identity field, first occurrence wins; hand the sink ONE complete in-order delivery only after the full scan; report the server's totalCount.
- Checkpoint discipline: the checkpoint file (single-line ISO 8601 UTC timestamp) advances to the newest delivered event timestamp ONLY after the sink accepted the delivery. A failed page fetch, a 404 project, or a sink that throws leaves the file byte-for-byte alone (and never creates it). An empty collection is a success: zero deliveries, no checkpoint write — per the docs it is NOT a 404, and a real 404 must raise.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — it runs a loopback mock Atlas; no real Atlas, no credentials, no sleeps. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 Events and Alerts resources (media type 2023-01-01)

official_sources.title: Return Events from One Project | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/events; query parameters eventType (array), clusterNames (array), pageNum (default 1), itemsPerPage (default 100, max 500), includeCount (default true); response {results, links, totalCount}; project read-only role suffices

official_sources.title: MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: listGroupEvents minDate/maxDate are format:date-time; eventType/excludedEventType arrays; response media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json; event views require created/eventTypeName/id with optional username; listGroupAlerts status query enum OPEN|TRACKING|CLOSED and alert views requiring alertConfigId/created/eventTypeName/id/status/updated; ApiError served as application/json

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: pagination rels self/next/previous with next omitted on the last page; empty lists are 200 with results: [] while nonexistent contexts 404; error envelope {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} with the RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND example

official_sources.title: Versioned Atlas Administration API Overview - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: Accept: application/vnd.atlas.<yyyy-mm-dd>+json dated media-type format and 406 semantics for wrong or missing version dates

research.language_rationale: The audit tooling this exporter feeds is a JVM shop. MongoDB publishes no official Java SDK for the Administration API (the Java driver is a data-plane client), so java.net.http.HttpClient against the documented versioned REST surface is the credible zero-dependency integration path.
